mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in RSAFS captures stronger household spending and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the signal is scored continuously.
failure_condition: Reject if the 3-month RSAFS growth coefficient is nonpositive in walk-forward tests or if top-quintile signal days fail to beat bottom-quintile days by at least 20 bp over 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0%, the increase in cash yield and equity discount rates reduces the present value of future earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0% after being below 5.0% in any of the prior 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean return.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals deteriorating labor demand and rising recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns after triggered UNRATE increases are positive in more than 55% of cases or if triggered returns are not below nontriggered returns.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average, rate-sensitive residential investment is contracting, reducing cyclical demand and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST breakdowns are followed by 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional median in more than half of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month momentum in INDPRO signals improving output, utilization, and goods-sector earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the walk-forward coefficient on 3-month INDPRO momentum is negative or if its rank information coefficient is below 0.03 over every 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation decelerates while FEDFUNDS remains restrictive, expected future policy easing rises and discount-rate pressure falls, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation crosses below its 12-month inflation rate while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered disinflation-with-restrictive-rates observations do not produce higher 60-day returns than other restrictive-rate observations, or if the interaction term is nonpositive.

mechanism_narrative: Positive PAYEMS growth combined with UNRATE below 4.0% identifies a soft-landing labor backdrop with income support and limited default risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive and UNRATE is below 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if 5-day forward S&P 500 returns under the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-below-4.0 condition are not above the unconditional 5-day return mean.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDPC1 growth while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% signals demand resilience that keeps policy restrictive for longer, preventing valuation multiple expansion and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth exceeds 3.0% while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered strong-growth restrictive-rate observations have mean 20-day returns above the unconditional mean or if the GDPC1-by-FEDFUNDS interaction predicts positive returns.
